title: Deparia mauiana
taxonomy: Deparia mauiana is a species within the genus Deparia, which is a group of ferns in the family Athyriaceae. This family is part of the larger division Polypodiophyta, which comprises all ferns. The Athyriaceae family is further divided into several genera, including Deparia. Deparia mauiana is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a relatively rare species within its genus.

intro: Deparia mauiana, also known as Mauian maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and slender stalks. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
